name: sim_weyl_group_bc2_root_system
scope_note: Shell-local probe: Weyl group W(B2) = Dih4 (order 8) and B2 root system. Tests: group order, root permutation, orthogonality, Coxeter relation (order 4), Cartan matrix asymmetry, Clifford reflections, Cayley graph, XGI hyperedges. z3 UNSAT: (s1*s2)^3=I is impossible for B2 (order is 4, not 3).

pytorch.reason: Represent B2 roots as 2D tensors; apply all 8 Weyl group elements; verify permutation closure and check long/short root lengths; confirm Coxeter relation (s1*s2)^4 = I numerically

pyg.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

z3.reason: UNSAT: (s1*s2)^3 = I for B2 is impossible (B2 has Coxeter order 4, not 3); encode as direct matrix constraint contradiction

cvc5.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

sympy.reason: Cartan matrix B2; compute simple reflections s_i(lambda) = lambda - <lambda,alpha_i^v>*alpha_i; verify (s1*s2)^4 = I symbolically; confirm (s1*s2)^3 != I

clifford.reason: B2 roots as grade-1 elements in Cl(2,0); reflections via Clifford sandwich -(alpha*v*alpha^{-1}); verify long root alpha=e1+e2 and short root alpha=e1 generate the correct reflection matrices

geomstats.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

e3nn.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

rustworkx.reason: Cayley graph of W(B2) = Dih4 with generators {s1, s2}: 8 nodes, verify it's the Dih4 Cayley graph structure (strongly connected, correct counts)

xgi.reason: Hyperedge {s1_idx, s2_idx, (s1*s2)^4_idx} recording the Coxeter relation as a 3-way constraint; hyperedge {long_root_1, long_root_2, short_root_1} encoding the mixed root-type triple

toponetx.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

gudhi.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

P1_pytorch_weyl_group_b2_order_8.reason: W(B2) = Dih4 has order 8; generated by {s1,s2} with Coxeter relation (s1s2)^4=e

P2_pytorch_b2_two_orbits_short_and_long.reason: B2 is not simply-laced: W(B2) has TWO orbits on roots — 4 short roots (orbit of e2) and 4 long roots (orbit of e1+e2). NOT transitive on all 8 roots (unlike A2/simply-laced).

P3_pytorch_weyl_b2_elements_orthogonal.reason: All W(B2) elements are orthogonal: M^T M = I, det = ±1

P4_pytorch_weyl_b2_permutes_roots.reason: Every W(B2) element maps each B2 root to another B2 root

P5_pytorch_b2_root_lengths_sqrt2_and_1.reason: B2: long roots length=sqrt(2), short roots length=1; ratio = sqrt(2) characteristic of B2

P6_pytorch_coxeter_relation_s1s2_order_4.reason: (s1*s2)^4 = I for B2; Coxeter order of the pair (s1,s2) is 4 (unlike 3 for A2)

P7_sympy_cartan_matrix_b2.reason: B2 Cartan matrix = [[2,-1],[-2,2]] (asymmetric: encodes short/long root distinction)

P8_sympy_coxeter_s1s2_order_4_symbolic.reason: (s1*s2)^4 = I for B2 verified symbolically with exact rational arithmetic

P9_clifford_reflections_map_b2_roots_to_.reason: Clifford reflection -alpha*v*alpha^{-1} (with alpha^{-1}=~alpha/|alpha|^2 for non-unit roots) maps each B2 root to another B2 root

N1_pytorch_s1s2_order_3_fails_for_b2.reason: (s1*s2)^3 != I for B2; the Coxeter order is 4 (not 3 as in A2); B2 is NOT A2

N2_pytorch_b2_order_not_6.reason: W(B2) has order 8, not 6; W(B2) != W(A2) (Dih4 != S3)

N3_z3_unsat_s1s2_order3_impossible_for_b.reason: UNSAT: (S1*S2)^3 has entry (0,0)=0 for B2, not 1 (identity); encoding both constraints is contradictory; (s1*s2)^3=I is impossible for B2

N4_sympy_s1s2_order_3_fails_symbolic.reason: (s1*s2)^3 != I for B2 confirmed symbolically; product is a 270-deg rotation, not identity

N5_pytorch_short_long_roots_different_le.reason: B2 has two root lengths: short=1, long=sqrt(2); unlike A2 where all roots have equal length

B1_pytorch_z2xz2_subgroup_commutes.reason: s_e1 and s_e2 commute (both are reflections across coordinate axes); Z2xZ2 = W(A1)xW(A1) subset W(B2)

B2_pytorch_z2xz2_subgroup_closed.reason: Z2xZ2 subgroup {e, s_e1, s_e2, s_e1*s_e2} is closed under multiplication (index-2 subgroup of Dih4)

B3_sympy_order_of_s1s2_is_exactly_4.reason: (s1*s2) has order exactly 4 in W(B2): (s1*s2)^4=I but (s1*s2)^2 != I

B4_rustworkx_b2_cayley_graph_strongly_co.reason: Cayley graph of W(B2) = Dih4 with generators {s1,s2}: 8 nodes, strongly connected

B5_xgi_coxeter_hyperedge_and_mixed_root_.reason: XGI hyperedges: {s1,s2,(s1s2)^4=e} for Coxeter, {long,long,short} for mixed root triple

B6_z3_sat_long_roots_same_length_consist.reason: SAT: encoding that long roots have length^2=2 is consistent; W(B2) transitivity on long roots
